title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Sergeant Spence and Chief Inspector Grieve meet with Rolly Claude at the police station to discuss the death of a man at the Stagg, who had registered under the name Enoch Yates. The police present several items found at the scene: a smashed watch face, a gold lighter engraved with initials, a gold lipstick tube, and heavy steel fire tongs bearing rust-brown stains.

Rolly explains his movements leading up to the incident. After receiving information from Beatrice Ripplecote regarding the status of a man named Robert Andes—who was believed to be dead in Africa but might still be alive—Rolly visited his uncle, the lawyer Jeremy Claude, at approximately 8:20 PM. Deciding not to wait for the cautious lawyer, Rolly went to the Stagg to confront the man himself.

Rolly arrived at the man's room between 8:30 and 8:40 PM. He attempted to imply the man was involved in blackmail, but the man remained amused, claiming he had information to sell and was expecting a "client" that night who would pay for proof of Robert Andes's death. Assuming the client was David Hunter, Rolly became angry and left the room around 9:00 PM. Rolly expresses regret for his arrogant behavior, noting the man seemed ecstatic about the upcoming meeting.

When shown the steel tongs, Rolly observes there was no fire in the room during his visit. Spence reveals that medical evidence shows the victim was struck from behind and then hit from above with the fireplace tool. Rolly expresses a desire for the man to still be alive so he could have obtained the information. Rolly admits he does not recognize the gold lighter or the lipstick tube.
